title: Strategy Definition
point: Strategy is the direction and scope of an organization over the long term.

title: Strategy Objective
point: Strategy achieves advantage through configuration of resources.

title: Strategy Environment
point: Strategy operates within a challenging environment.

title: Strategy Market Focus
point: Strategy aims to meet market needs.

title: Strategy Stakeholder Focus
point: Strategy fulfills stakeholder expectations.

title: Corporate Strategy
point: Corporate strategy highlights issues vital to the organization.

title: Performance Measurement
point: Corporate strategy measures corporate performance.

title: Future Competencies
point: Corporate strategy envisions competencies required for future sustenance.

title: SAIL Position
point: SAIL is one of the largest industrial entities in India.

title: Leading Producer
point: SAIL is the leading steel producer in India.

title: Product Strength
point: SAIL possesses a diverse range of quality steel products.

title: Manpower Strength
point: SAIL has a large pool of technical and professional manpower.

title: Integration Strength
point: SAIL is 100% integrated in iron ore.

title: Distribution Network
point: SAIL has a nationwide distribution network.

title: Multiple Locations
point: SAIL operates multiple plant locations across India.

title: Expansion Potential
point: SAIL can expand to around 48 million tonnes of crude steel.

title: Steel Industry Trend
point: The steel sector experienced challenges and growth opportunities over two decades.

title: Industry Evolution
point: Indian steel industry evolution occurred in two distinct stages.

title: Stage One
point: First stage was the Planned Self-Reliance economy till the 1980s.

title: Stage Two
point: Second stage was the transition decade of the 1990s and post-liberalization era.

title: Industry Impact
point: Business profiles were shaped by different economic phases.

title: Mindset Influence
point: Industry evolution influenced organizational mindsets.

title: Behavioral Impact
point: Industry evolution affected behavioral characteristics of steel companies.

title: Post-1990 Changes
point: Post-1990 economic reforms transformed the Indian economy.

title: Global Integration
point: Steel producers had to move into the global economy.

title: Operational Choices
point: SAIL must evaluate various operational choices for decision-making.

title: Environmental Constraints
point: External environment imposes constraints on business decisions.

title: Performance Outcomes
point: Business choices influence profitability and competitiveness.

title: Steel Reform
point: Iron and steel were removed from industries reserved for the public sector.

title: Licensing Reform
point: Licensing for capacity creation and investment was removed.

title: FDI Reform
point: Automatic approval of foreign investment up to 100% equity was allowed.

title: Strategy Formulation
point: Strategy formulation analyzes the business environment before action planning.

title: Continuous Improvement
point: Strategy formulation follows a system of continuous improvement.

title: Analysis Linkage
point: Output of one analysis becomes input for the next stage.

title: Vision and Mission
point: Vision and mission guide business strategy direction.

title: Strategic Direction
point: Vision provides organizational direction.

title: Strategic Priorities
point: Mission helps establish priorities.

title: Alignment Check
point: Organizational conditions are examined for alignment with vision.

title: Opportunity Analysis
point: Opportunities are analyzed for feasibility.

title: Scenario Analysis
point: Scenarios are evaluated based on organizational position.

title: Internal Analysis
point: Internal analysis identifies strengths and weaknesses.

title: External Analysis
point: External analysis identifies opportunities and threats.

title: SWOT Analysis
point: SWOT includes strengths

title: Strategic Options
point: Strategic options are formulated after analysis.

title: Alternative Projection
point: Strategic options project multiple possibilities.

title: Option Selection
point: Best strategic option is selected after careful evaluation.

title: Business Strategy
point: A broad business strategy illustrates overall direction of action.

title: Functional Breakdown
point: Business strategy is divided into functional components.

title: Functional Areas
point: Operational
column6: Finance and Marketing are key strategic components.

title: Action Plans
point: Each function develops detailed action plans.

title: Corporate Planning
point: SAIL used this approach for a series of corporate plans.

title: Industry Adaptation
point: Corporate plans reflected changing steel industry scenarios.

title: Planning Culture
point: SAIL has always believed in structured planning.

title: Planning Impact
point: Planning culture contributed to SAIL's growth.

title: National Impact
point: Steel sector growth contributed to national development.

title: Linkages
point: Steel industry has strong backward and forward linkages.

title: Planning Initiation
point: Formal long-range planning began in SAIL in 1986.

title: Corporate Plan 1987
point: The first corporate plan document was published in 1987.

title: Corporate Plan 1992
point: The second corporate plan document was published in 1992.

title: Planning Horizon
point: Early corporate plans had a perspective of around 15 years.

title: Milestones
point: Corporate plans included 5-year milestones.

title: Reviews
point: Corporate plans underwent reviews every five years.

title: Business Environment
point: Changing business environment required strategic adaptation.

title: Restructuring Need
point: Fundamental business changes required restructuring instead of traditional growth plans.

title: Corporate Plan 2000
point: Corporate Plan-2000 was released in May 1987.

title: Plan Duration
point: Corporate Plan-2000 extended up to the year 2000 AD.

title: First Corporate Plan
point: Corporate Plan-2000 was SAIL's first corporate plan.

title: Milestone Structure
point: Corporate Plan-2000 followed 15-year planning with 5-year milestones.

title: Market Share Goal
point: Corporate Plan-2000 aimed to retain 60% market share.

title: Modernization Focus
point: Corporate Plan-2000 emphasized modernization.

title: Technology Upgrade
point: Technological upgradation was a key objective.

title: Capacity Improvement
point: Modernization aimed to improve production capacity.

title: Productivity Improvement
point: Modernization aimed to improve productivity.

title: DSP Modernization
point: DSP was selected for initial modernization.

title: RSP Modernization
point: RSP was selected for initial modernization.

title: BSL Modernization
point: BSL was selected for initial modernization.

title: Corporate Plan 2005
point: Corporate Plan-2005 was issued in February 1992.

title: Plan Horizon
point: Corporate Plan-2005 extended up to 2005 AD.

title: Economic Reform Context
point: Corporate Plan-2005 was drafted after 1991-92 reforms.

title: Financial Target
point: Corporate Plan-2005 targeted PAT/NW ratio of at least 12%.

title: PAT/NW Ratio
point: PAT/NW refers to Profit After Tax to Net Worth ratio.

title: Decentralized Planning
point: Corporate Plan-2005 adopted decentralized planning.

title: Unit Perspective Plans
point: Unit Perspective Plans were prepared under CP-2005.

title: Conflict Resolution
point: Chief Executives meetings resolved conflicting objectives.

title: Investment Allocation
point: Inter-plant investment allocation conflicts were addressed.

title: Product Mix Decisions
point: Product mix conflicts were resolved across units.

title: Turnaround Plan
point: Turnaround Plan was launched in August 1998.

title: Plan Duration
point: Turnaround Plan covered up to 2003 AD.

title: Profitability Period
point: SAIL earned consistent profits from 1984-85 to 1997-98.

title: Financial Strain
point: SAIL faced financial strain during 1998-99.

title: Turnaround Period
point: Turnaround efforts continued from 1998-99 to 2002-03.

title: Historic Achievement
point: SAIL scripted the largest corporate turnaround in India.

title: Turnaround Initiative
point: Restoring financial foundation was a turnaround initiative.

title: Turnaround Initiative
point: Organization restructuring was a turnaround initiative.

title: Turnaround Initiative
point: Marketing initiatives formed part of turnaround efforts.

title: Turnaround Initiative
point: Cost reduction was a key turnaround measure.

title: Turnaround Initiative
point: Manpower rightsizing was a turnaround measure.

title: Turnaround Success
point: SAIL successfully turned around by the last quarter of 2002-03.

title: Growth Readiness
point: SAIL became ready for future growth plans after turnaround.

title: Corporate Plan 2012
point: Corporate Plan-2012 was launched in July 2004.

title: Plan Horizon
point: Corporate Plan-2012 extended up to 2012 AD.

title: Strategic Orientation
point: SAIL focused on building a robust organization.

title: Strong Fundamentals
point: Corporate Plan-2012 emphasized strong fundamentals.

title: Hot Metal Target
point: Initial hot metal target under CP-2012 was 23 million tonnes.

title: Saleable Steel Target
point: Initial saleable steel target was 20 million tonnes.

title: MEP Plan
point: A company-wide Modernization-cum-Expansion Plan was developed.

title: MEP Coverage
point: MEP included plants and mines.

title: CPFR Finding
point: CPFR indicated higher capacity potential.

title: Revised Hot Metal Target
point: Hot metal capacity could reach 26 million tonnes.

title: Revised Saleable Steel Target
point: Saleable steel capacity could reach 23 million tonnes.

title: Vision 2030
point: Vision 2030 is SAIL's medium- and long-term business strategy.

title: Vision 2030 Target
point: Vision 2030 targets 50 million tonnes of hot metal.

title: Vision 2030 Steel Target
point: Vision 2030 targets 48 million tonnes of crude steel.

title: Global Aspiration
point: Vision 2030 aims to place SAIL among top global metals and mining companies.

title: Customer Base
point: Vision 2030 focuses on broadening the customer base.

title: Export Focus
point: Vision 2030 seeks increased exports.

title: Retail Focus
point: Vision 2030 emphasizes retail sales growth.

title: De-Risking Strategy
point: Forward integration is used to de-risk business operations.

title: SWOT-Based Planning
point: Vision 2030 strategies are based on SWOT analysis.

title: Market Leadership
point: Market leadership is a key strategic area.

title: Global Orientation
point: Market leadership is pursued with a global orientation.

title: Product Mix Alignment
point: Product mix should align with industry trends.

title: Input Integration
point: Complete integration of key raw materials is targeted.

title: Techno-Economic Improvement
point: Improving techno-economic parameters is a strategic goal.

title: Efficient Deployment
point: Efficient manpower deployment is emphasized.

title: Infrastructure Dominance
point: SAIL aims to retain dominance in infrastructure sector.

title: Construction Dominance
point: SAIL aims to retain dominance in construction sector.

title: Value Added Products
point: SAIL seeks strong presence in value-added products.

title: New Products
point: Several new products are envisioned.

title: New Mills
point: Several new mills are planned.

title: Market Expansion
point: New products help expand customer base.

title: Alternate Smelting
point: Alternate smelting processes are targeted for hot metal production.

title: Actual Hot Metal
point: Actual hot metal production in 2016-17 was 15.7 mtpa.

title: Expanded Hot Metal
point: Expanded hot metal capacity target is 23.5 mtpa.

title: Actual Crude Steel
point: Actual crude steel production in 2016-17 was 14.4 mtpa.

title: Expanded Crude Steel
point: Expanded crude steel capacity target is 21.4 mtpa.

title: Actual Saleable Steel
point: Actual saleable steel production in 2016-17 was 13.8 mtpa.

title: Expanded Saleable Steel
point: Expanded saleable steel target is 20.23 mtpa.

title: New Mines
point: New mines will support future hot metal production.

title: Beneficiation Units
point: Beneficiation units will be developed with new mines.

title: Pellet Capacity
point: Planned pellet capacity is around 15 million tonnes.

title: Dedicated Rail Tracks
point: SAIL may own dedicated rail tracks for logistics.

title: Specialized Wagons
point: Specialized wagons are planned for logistics efficiency.

title: New Ports
point: Development of eastern coast ports is planned.

title: Inland Waterways
point: SAIL plans to explore inland waterways.

title: Logistics Management
point: Efficient logistics support incremental steel volumes.

title: Land Utilization
point: Continual improvement in land utilization is targeted.

title: Steel Technology
point: Continuous improvement in steelmaking technology is targeted.

title: Productivity Focus
point: Manpower productivity improvement is a key goal.

title: Energy Management
point: Energy management is a priority area.

title: Sustainability
point: Sustainability is a target area for excellence.

title: HR Strategy
point: HR strategy focuses on organizational restructuring.

title: Employee Engagement
point: Employee engagement must align with growth plans.

title: Strategic Alliances
point: SAIL has formed domestic and international strategic alliances.

title: Raw Material JV
point: International Coal Ventures Ltd. supports raw material security.

title: ICVL Partners
point: ICVL is a JV with CIL

title: ICVL Objective
point: ICVL acquires coal assets globally.

title: Energy Alliance
point: SAIL has power JV partnerships with NTPC and DVC.

title: Energy Objective
point: Power JVs support captive consumption.

title: Cement JV
point: Bhilai Jaypee Cement Ltd. is SAIL's cement JV.

title: Cement Capacity
point: Cement plant capacity is 2.1 million tonnes per annum.

title: Grinding Unit
point: Grinding unit is located at Bhilai.

title: Clinkering Unit
point: Clinkering unit is located at Satna.

title: Wagon JV
point: SAIL formed a JV for specialized wagon manufacturing.

title: E-Portal JV
point: Tata Steel is SAIL's partner in e-commerce activities.

title: E-Portal Objective
point: E-portal JV promotes e-commerce in steel.

title: Steel Processing JV
point: BMW Industries Ltd. is a steel processing JV partner.

title: Steel Processing Partner
point: Prime Gold Group is a steel processing JV partner.

title: Strategy Formula
point: Vision plus analysis plus execution equals strategy.

title: Vision 2030 Roadmap
point: Vision 2030 defines SAIL's roadmap beyond current expansion.

title: Planning Culture
point: Structured planning is a hallmark of SAIL.

title: Strategic Success
point: SAIL adapted strategy to changing business environments.

title: Corporate Planning
point: 1987 and 1992 were the two early corporate plan years.

title: Turnaround Period
point: 1998-2003 is remembered as SAIL's turnaround phase.

title: Vision 2030 Targets
point: 50 MT hot metal and 48 MT crude steel are key Vision 2030 figures.
